Title: Valid credentials authenticate a clinician successfully
Preconditions: OpenMRS RefApp reachable over HTTPS; a clinician account exists and is active
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to the login page | 2. Enter a valid username | 3. Enter the correct password | 4. Select a session location | 5. Click Log In
Expected_Result: User is authenticated, redirected to the home dashboard, and a server-side session is established

Title: Login fails with a wrong password and no detail leaked
Preconditions: A valid username exists; user is on the login page
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a valid username | 2. Enter an incorrect password | 3. Select a session location | 4. Click Log In
Expected_Result: Login is rejected with a generic invalid-credentials message that does not reveal whether the username or password was wrong; no session is created

Title: Login rejected for a non-existent username
Preconditions: User is on the login page; the username does not exist
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a username that does not exist | 2. Enter any password | 3. Select a session location | 4. Click Log In
Expected_Result: Login is rejected with the same generic error used for a wrong password, preventing username enumeration

Title: Empty username and password are rejected
Preconditions: User is on the login page
Test_Steps: 1. Leave username blank | 2. Leave password blank | 3. Click Log In
Expected_Result: Form validation blocks submission and no authentication attempt is made against the server

Title: Password complexity policy enforced on password change
Preconditions: Logged in as a user permitted to change own password; password policy configured (min length, mixed case, digit, symbol)
Test_Steps: 1. Open Change Password | 2. Enter current password | 3. Enter a candidate new password | 4. Confirm new password | 5. Submit | 6. Repeat for each candidate in the data set
Test_Data: Cases: short=Ab1!; no-digit=Abcdefg!; no-symbol=Abcdefg1; no-upper=abcdefg1!; valid=Abcd1234!
Expected_Result: Each non-compliant candidate is rejected with a policy message; only the compliant candidate is accepted

Title: Password minimum length boundary values
Preconditions: Logged in user on Change Password screen; minimum length configured at 8
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a new password of length 7 | 2. Submit a new password of length 8 | 3. Submit a new password of length 9
Expected_Result: Length 7 is rejected; lengths 8 and 9 are accepted, confirming the boundary is inclusive at the minimum

Title: New password cannot equal current password
Preconditions: Logged in user on Change Password screen; password reuse prevention enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Enter current password | 2. Enter the same value as the new password | 3. Confirm | 4. Submit
Expected_Result: Change is rejected with a message that the new password must differ from the current one

Title: Account locks after configured consecutive failed logins
Preconditions: Lockout threshold configured (e.g., 5 failures); a valid test account exists
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt login with wrong password repeatedly up to threshold-1 | 2. Observe account still allows attempts | 3. Submit one more wrong password reaching the threshold | 4. Attempt login again with the correct password
Expected_Result: After the threshold of failures the account transitions to a locked state and even correct credentials are refused until lockout expires or an admin unlocks

Title: Lockout counter resets after a successful login
Preconditions: Lockout threshold configured; account is below threshold
Test_Steps: 1. Submit threshold-1 failed logins | 2. Submit the correct password and log in | 3. Log out | 4. Submit one failed login | 5. Inspect that the counter restarted
Expected_Result: After a successful login the failed-attempt counter resets to zero so a subsequent single failure does not lock the account

Title: Locked account auto-unlocks after the configured window
Preconditions: Account is currently locked; auto-unlock window configured
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm account is locked | 2. Wait until the configured unlock window elapses | 3. Attempt login with the correct password
Expected_Result: After the unlock window passes, login with the correct password succeeds and the account state returns to active

Title: Session cookie is marked HttpOnly and Secure
Preconditions: User can authenticate over HTTPS; browser dev tools or a proxy available for inspection
Test_Steps: 1. Log in successfully | 2. Inspect the Set-Cookie response header for the session cookie | 3. Check the cookie attributes
Expected_Result: The session cookie carries HttpOnly and Secure attributes and an appropriate SameSite value, preventing script access and plaintext transmission

Title: Session ID is regenerated upon successful login
Preconditions: Pre-login session identifier captured; intercepting proxy available
Test_Steps: 1. Load the login page and record the pre-auth session ID | 2. Submit valid credentials | 3. Record the post-auth session ID | 4. Compare the two
Expected_Result: The session identifier changes after authentication, preventing session fixation

Title: Idle session times out after the configured period
Preconditions: Session timeout configured; user is logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Leave the session idle beyond the configured timeout | 3. Attempt to access a protected page
Expected_Result: The idle session is invalidated and the user is redirected to login when accessing protected content

Title: Logout invalidates the server-side session
Preconditions: User is logged in with an active session
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Capture the session cookie | 3. Click Log Out | 4. Replay a protected request reusing the captured session cookie
Expected_Result: After logout the server rejects the replayed session cookie and the protected request returns an unauthenticated response

Title: Concurrent sessions handled per policy
Preconditions: Concurrent-session policy defined; one account available
Test_Steps: 1. Log in from browser A | 2. Log in with the same account from browser B | 3. Perform an action in browser A | 4. Observe whether session A remains valid
Expected_Result: System behaves per the documented concurrent-session policy (either both valid or the earlier session invalidated) with no data corruption

Title: Role-restricted privilege blocks unauthorized clinical action
Preconditions: A user with a Receptionist role lacking the privilege to enter clinical observations is logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as the receptionist | 2. Navigate to a patient dashboard | 3. Attempt to open the clinical observation entry form
Expected_Result: The clinical entry action is hidden or denied with an authorization error and no observation can be created

Title: Privilege grant enables the previously blocked action
Preconditions: Admin can edit roles; receptionist role currently lacks the clinical privilege
Test_Steps: 1. As admin, grant the clinical-entry privilege to the role | 2. Log in as the receptionist | 3. Re-attempt the clinical observation entry
Expected_Result: After the privilege is granted the user can perform the action, confirming authorization is privilege-driven

Title: Direct object reference to another patient record is denied
Preconditions: Logged in as a clinician whose access is scoped; two patient UUIDs known, one outside the user scope
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as the scoped clinician | 2. Note an in-scope patient UUID | 3. Manually edit the URL to an out-of-scope patient UUID | 4. Submit the request
Expected_Result: Access to the out-of-scope record is denied or filtered; the unauthorized PHI is not returned

Title: REST API rejects access to an unauthorized patient resource
Preconditions: An API token for a low-privilege user; a patient UUID the user cannot access
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate to the REST API as the low-privilege user | 2. Issue GET to the patient resource for the unauthorized UUID | 3. Inspect status and body
Expected_Result: API returns a 403 or 404 without exposing the patient demographics or PHI of the unauthorized record

Title: Sequential numeric ID manipulation does not expose other encounters
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; an encounter belonging to another provider exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open an in-scope encounter and note its identifier pattern | 2. Modify the identifier to reference another provider's encounter | 3. Request the modified resource
Expected_Result: The system denies the manipulated reference and returns no encounter content the user is not authorized to view

Title: SQL injection payload in patient search is neutralized
Preconditions: Logged in with patient-search privilege; design-only test against a controlled non-production instance
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient search | 2. Enter a SQL meta-character payload in the name field | 3. Submit the search | 4. Observe results and logs
Expected_Result: Input is treated as a literal search string; no extra records are returned, no SQL error is surfaced, and parameterized queries prevent injection

Title: Injection payload in REST query parameter is safely handled
Preconditions: Valid API token; non-production controlled instance
Test_Steps: 1. Issue a patient search REST call with an injection payload in the q parameter | 2. Inspect response status and body
Expected_Result: API returns a normal empty or filtered result with no database error, stack trace, or unintended data exposure

Title: Oversized input field is rejected at the boundary
Preconditions: Logged in registration clerk; field max length defined (e.g., 50 chars for given name)
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a name of exactly the maximum length | 2. Save | 3. Enter a name of maximum length plus one | 4. Save
Expected_Result: The maximum-length value is accepted and the over-limit value is rejected or truncated per spec, preventing buffer or storage issues

Title: Special and Unicode characters are stored without corruption
Preconditions: Logged in registration clerk
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient with accented and non-Latin characters in the name | 2. Save | 3. Reopen the patient record
Expected_Result: The characters are stored and rendered correctly without corruption, double-encoding, or injection side effects

Title: Stored script in a free-text field is neutralized on display
Preconditions: Logged in with privilege to enter a free-text note; controlled non-production instance
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a script payload into a free-text note field | 2. Save the note | 3. Reopen the record that renders the note
Expected_Result: The payload is HTML-encoded or stripped on render; no script executes and the literal text or sanitized value is shown

Title: Reflected input in search results is encoded
Preconditions: Logged in with search privilege; controlled non-production instance
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a search containing an HTML/script payload | 2. Observe how the search term is reflected on the results page
Expected_Result: The reflected term is output-encoded so the markup is displayed as text and no event handler or image error fires

Title: Attribute-context payload does not break out of attribute
Preconditions: Logged in user; field rendered into an HTML attribute; controlled instance
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a payload designed to break out of an attribute context | 2. Save | 3. Inspect rendered HTML source
Expected_Result: The value is attribute-encoded so it cannot escape the attribute boundary and no event handler is injected

Title: Security response headers are present
Preconditions: Application reachable over HTTPS; proxy or dev tools available
Test_Steps: 1. Request the application home page over HTTPS | 2. Inspect response headers for CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, and HSTS
Expected_Result: Recommended security headers are present, mitigating clickjacking, MIME sniffing, and enforcing HTTPS

Title: HTTP requests are redirected to HTTPS
Preconditions: Application deployed with TLS and HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect configured
Test_Steps: 1. Issue a plain HTTP request to the login URL | 2. Observe the redirect | 3. Confirm the final URL uses HTTPS
Expected_Result: The request is redirected with a 301/302 to the HTTPS equivalent and credentials are never sent over plaintext HTTP

Title: TLS configuration disallows weak protocols and ciphers
Preconditions: Access to the TLS endpoint; design-only configuration review
Test_Steps: 1. Review the negotiated TLS version and cipher suites | 2. Confirm TLS 1.2 or higher only | 3. Confirm deprecated protocols are disabled
Expected_Result: Only TLS 1.2+ with strong cipher suites is offered; SSLv3, TLS 1.0 and 1.1 and weak ciphers are disabled

Title: Patient identifiers are not exposed in URL query strings
Preconditions: Logged in clinician viewing a patient chart
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient chart | 2. Inspect the browser address bar and any AJAX request URLs | 3. Check whether PHI such as name, MRN, or SSN appears as a query parameter
Expected_Result: Patient PHI is not transmitted in URL query strings; opaque UUIDs are used and no sensitive demographic data leaks via URLs or referrers

Title: Server logs do not record PHI or credentials
Preconditions: Access to application and server logs in a controlled environment; ability to generate activity
Test_Steps: 1. Perform a login and a patient search | 2. Inspect application logs and access logs | 3. Search logs for passwords, tokens, MRN, SSN, and full names
Expected_Result: Logs contain no plaintext passwords, session tokens, or PHI; sensitive values are masked or omitted

Title: Browser back button does not expose PHI after logout
Preconditions: User has viewed a patient chart and then logged out
Test_Steps: 1. Log in and open a patient chart | 2. Log out | 3. Press the browser Back button | 4. Observe the page content
Expected_Result: Cached PHI is not served after logout; the user is redirected to login and no patient data is rendered

Title: Cache-control headers prevent caching of PHI pages
Preconditions: Logged in user; proxy or dev tools available
Test_Steps: 1. Open a page containing PHI | 2. Inspect response headers for Cache-Control and Pragma
Expected_Result: PHI-bearing responses carry no-store/no-cache directives so sensitive content is not retained by browsers or intermediaries

Title: Stored password is hashed and salted, never plaintext
Preconditions: Read access to the user credential store in a controlled non-production environment
Test_Steps: 1. Create a user with a known password | 2. Query the stored credential record | 3. Inspect the stored value and any salt column
Expected_Result: The stored value is a one-way salted hash (not reversible, not the plaintext), with a per-user salt and a strong algorithm

Title: Two users with identical passwords have different stored hashes
Preconditions: Read access to the credential store in a controlled environment
Test_Steps: 1. Create user A and user B with the same password | 2. Query both stored hashes | 3. Compare them
Expected_Result: The two stored hashes differ, confirming a unique per-user salt prevents hash correlation and rainbow-table attacks

Title: State-changing request without a valid CSRF token is rejected
Preconditions: Logged in user with a valid session; controlled non-production instance; proxy available
Test_Steps: 1. Capture a legitimate state-changing POST and note its anti-CSRF token | 2. Replay the request with the token removed or altered | 3. Observe the response
Test_Data: POST save patient note without CSRF token
Expected_Result: The request is rejected and no state change occurs, confirming CSRF protection on state-changing endpoints

Title: CSRF token is unique per session and not predictable
Preconditions: Two separate authenticated sessions available
Test_Steps: 1. Capture the CSRF token from session 1 | 2. Capture the token from session 2 | 3. Compare values and assess randomness
Expected_Result: Tokens differ between sessions and show sufficient entropy, so a token cannot be guessed or reused across sessions

Title: Server errors do not leak stack traces or internals
Preconditions: Ability to trigger an error condition on a controlled instance
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a malformed request that triggers a server error | 2. Inspect the error response shown to the user
Test_Data: Malformed=invalid JSON body to a REST endpoint
Expected_Result: A generic error message is shown with no stack trace, framework version, SQL, or file path disclosure

Title: 404 and 500 pages do not reveal technology fingerprint
Preconditions: Application reachable; ability to request a non-existent path
Test_Steps: 1. Request a non-existent URL | 2. Inspect the 404 response body and headers | 3. Trigger a 500 and inspect
Expected_Result: Error pages are generic and Server/X-Powered-By headers do not disclose exact framework or version details

Title: Successful login is recorded in the security audit log
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; access to audit records
Test_Steps: 1. Log in successfully as a clinician | 2. Query the audit log for the login event
Expected_Result: An audit entry records the successful login with user identity, timestamp, and source, supporting accountability

Title: Failed login attempts are audited
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; access to audit records
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a failed login | 2. Query the audit log for the failed-login event
Expected_Result: An audit entry records the failed attempt with username, timestamp, and outcome, enabling intrusion detection

Title: Patient record access is logged for PHI accountability
Preconditions: Audit logging for record access enabled; access to audit records
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as a clinician | 2. Open a patient chart | 3. Query the audit log for a record-access event
Expected_Result: An audit entry captures who accessed which patient record and when, satisfying HIPAA accountability for PHI access

Title: Privilege and role changes are audited
Preconditions: Admin can modify roles; audit logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. As admin, change a user's role or privilege | 2. Query the audit log for the change event
Test_Data: Admin grants Add Observations to Receptionist
Expected_Result: An audit entry records the privilege change including actor, target, before/after, and timestamp

Title: Audit log entries are tamper-evident and read-only to clinical users
Preconditions: Logged in as a non-admin clinical user
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to delete or modify an existing audit log entry through the UI or API
Expected_Result: The clinical user cannot alter or delete audit records; modification is denied and the attempt itself is logged

Title: Database backups containing PHI are encrypted
Preconditions: Access to backup configuration in a controlled environment; encryption-at-rest policy defined
Test_Steps: 1. Review backup storage configuration | 2. Confirm encryption-at-rest is enabled for database and file backups | 3. Verify key management is documented
Expected_Result: Backups containing PHI are encrypted at rest with managed keys, meeting the data-at-rest protection requirement

Title: Sensitive configuration secrets are not stored in plaintext
Preconditions: Access to deployment configuration in a controlled environment
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect configuration files and environment for database passwords and API keys | 2. Confirm secrets are externalized and protected
Expected_Result: Secrets are not committed in plaintext; they are stored in a secret manager or protected store, not in readable config

Title: Unauthenticated REST request is rejected
Preconditions: REST endpoint available; no credentials supplied
Test_Steps: 1. Issue a GET to a protected REST resource with no auth header or session | 2. Inspect the status code
Expected_Result: The API responds with 401 Unauthorized and returns no patient data

Title: Expired or revoked token is rejected
Preconditions: An expired or revoked API token is available
Test_Steps: 1. Issue a protected REST request using the expired/revoked token | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: The API rejects the request with 401 and the stale credential cannot access any resource

Title: SMART on FHIR scope limits returned resources
Preconditions: SMART on FHIR app authorized with a narrow scope (e.g., patient/Observation.read only)
Test_Steps: 1. Authorize the app with patient/Observation.read scope | 2. Request an Observation resource | 3. Request a MedicationRequest resource
Expected_Result: Observation read succeeds while the out-of-scope MedicationRequest is denied, enforcing least-privilege scopes

Title: FHIR Patient.read enforces compartment authorization
Preconditions: SMART app authorized for patient context A; resource B belongs to another patient
Test_Steps: 1. Launch in patient context A | 2. Request Patient/A | 3. Request Patient/B outside the authorized compartment
Expected_Result: Patient/A is returned; Patient/B is denied, confirming the FHIR compartment boundary is enforced

Title: FHIR server requires TLS for all requests
Preconditions: HAPI FHIR endpoint configured for TLS
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt a FHIR request over plain HTTP | 2. Attempt the same over HTTPS
Expected_Result: Plain HTTP is refused or redirected to HTTPS; only encrypted FHIR traffic is accepted

Title: HL7 inbound interface authenticates the sending application
Preconditions: An HL7 v2 MLLP interface configured to require sender authentication/allowlist
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT message from an unlisted sending application | 2. Send the same from an allowlisted sender
Expected_Result: Messages from unauthorized senders are rejected with an appropriate ACK error while allowlisted senders are accepted

Title: HL7 PHI in transit is protected by TLS or VPN
Preconditions: HL7 channel configuration available for review
Test_Steps: 1. Review the HL7 transport configuration | 2. Confirm MLLP runs over TLS or a secured tunnel
Expected_Result: HL7 messages carrying PHI traverse an encrypted channel, never plaintext MLLP over an untrusted network

Title: Privilege combinations evaluated across roles
Preconditions: Multiple roles and resource actions defined; test matrix prepared
Test_Steps: 1. For each role-action pair in the pairwise set, log in with the role | 2. Attempt the action | 3. Record allow/deny | 4. Compare with the access matrix
Expected_Result: Each role-action outcome matches the documented access-control matrix with no unexpected allow results

Title: MFA challenge required when enabled
Preconditions: MFA enabled for the account on the omiiCARE platform
Test_Steps: 1. Enter valid username and password | 2. Submit | 3. Observe the MFA challenge | 4. Enter a valid one-time code
Expected_Result: After primary credentials the user is prompted for the second factor and is only granted access after a valid code

Title: Invalid MFA code is rejected and limited
Preconditions: MFA enabled; user passed primary authentication
Test_Steps: 1. Complete primary authentication | 2. Enter an incorrect OTP repeatedly
Expected_Result: Invalid codes are rejected, attempts are rate-limited, and access is not granted without a valid second factor

Title: File upload restricts type and scans content
Preconditions: Logged in user with attachment upload privilege; controlled instance
Test_Steps: 1. Upload an allowed image file | 2. Upload a disallowed executable file | 3. Upload a file with a spoofed extension
Expected_Result: Allowed types succeed; disallowed and spoofed files are rejected based on content-type validation, preventing malicious upload

Title: Rate limiting throttles excessive requests
Preconditions: API rate-limit policy configured on the platform
Test_Steps: 1. Send requests within the allowed rate | 2. Exceed the configured request rate within the window | 3. Observe responses
Expected_Result: Requests within the limit succeed; requests exceeding the limit receive 429 Too Many Requests, mitigating brute-force and abuse

Title: Forced browsing to admin pages is blocked for non-admins
Preconditions: Logged in as a non-admin clinical user
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as the non-admin user | 2. Directly navigate to an administration URL
Expected_Result: The admin page is not rendered; the user receives an authorization error and cannot view or alter admin settings

Title: Cookie without SameSite is flagged in design review
Preconditions: Cookie policy matrix defined for Lax, Strict, and None contexts
Test_Steps: 1. For each SameSite configuration, inspect cookie behavior on cross-site navigation | 2. Compare against the policy matrix
Expected_Result: Each configuration matches the intended policy; SameSite=None is only permitted with Secure, mitigating CSRF and leakage

Title: Time-boxed exploratory probe of authentication and session flows
Preconditions: Controlled non-production instance; tester with valid low-privilege account; intercepting proxy
Test_Steps: 1. Explore login, password reset, and session handling for inconsistent error messages, weak token handling, and parameter tampering for 60 minutes | 2. Record observations and reproducible issues
Expected_Result: Findings are documented with reproduction steps; any weakness in auth, session, or error handling is logged for triage

Title: Password reset does not confirm account existence
Preconditions: Self-service password reset enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Request a reset for a known existing email | 2. Request a reset for a non-existent email | 3. Compare the on-screen responses
Expected_Result: Both requests return the same neutral confirmation message, preventing account enumeration via the reset flow

Title: Reset token is single-use and time-limited
Preconditions: A valid reset token was issued
Test_Steps: 1. Use the reset token to set a new password | 2. Attempt to reuse the same token | 3. Wait past expiry and attempt an unused token
Expected_Result: The token works once, is rejected on reuse, and an expired token is refused, limiting the reset window

Title: Login error messaging is accessible without leaking detail
Preconditions: Screen reader available; user on the login page
Test_Steps: 1. Submit invalid credentials | 2. Verify the error is announced by the screen reader and associated with the form
Expected_Result: The generic error is programmatically associated and announced to assistive tech while still not revealing which field was wrong

Title: Bulk export endpoint enforces per-record authorization
Preconditions: FHIR bulk data export configured; user with scoped access
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate a bulk export as a scoped user | 2. Inspect the exported resource set
Expected_Result: The export contains only records within the user's authorized scope; no out-of-scope patient resources are included

Title: Numeric vital-sign field rejects out-of-range and non-numeric input
Preconditions: Logged in clinician on an observation form; allowed range defined for a vital
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a value below the minimum | 2. Enter the minimum | 3. Enter the maximum | 4. Enter above the maximum | 5. Enter a non-numeric value
Expected_Result: Below-min, above-max, and non-numeric values are rejected; min and max are accepted, protecting data integrity and patient safety

Title: Audit records include sufficient detail for forensic review
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; access to audit storage
Test_Steps: 1. Perform a sensitive action | 2. Inspect the resulting audit record fields
Expected_Result: Each audit record includes actor identity, action, target object, timestamp, and source address, supporting forensic reconstruction
